weird_specifics.label: 诡异细节
weird_specifics.seed: On February 29th, 2037, Dr. Okonkwo discovered that earthworms in Lot 7B of her laboratory were singing at exactly 440 Hz. She measured it 11 times. The frequency never varied. She published her findings in the Quarterly Review of Annelid Acoustics, which had 23 subscribers.

chain: On February 29th, 2037, Dr. Okonkwo discovered that earthworms in Lot 7B of her laboratory were singing at exactly 440 Hz. She measured it 11 times. The frequency never varied. She published her findings in the Quarterly Review of Annelid Acoustics, which had 23 subscribers.

In 2037, Dr. Okonkwo found that earthworms in a specific lab area were producing sounds at exactly 440 Hz. She tested this frequency repeatedly and found it remained perfectly consistent. Her research ended up in a specialized journal that only had 23 people subscribed to it.

Back in 2037, Dr. Okonkwo discovered something odd happening with earthworms in one particular section of her laboratory - they were making sounds that measured precisely 440 Hz. She ran test after test to verify the frequency, and every single time it came back the same without any variation. The whole bizarre phenomenon eventually got published in a very niche academic journal that had just 23 subscribers worldwide.

dark.seed: The last customer at the all-night diner ordered coffee and sat looking at a photograph for four hours. When the waitress finally asked if he was okay, he said 'I'm trying to remember if this is my daughter or someone else's.' He left the photograph on the table. Nobody claimed it.

chain: The last customer at the all-night diner ordered coffee and sat looking at a photograph for four hours. When the waitress finally asked if he was okay, he said 'I'm trying to remember if this is my daughter or someone else's.' He left the photograph on the table. Nobody claimed it.

The final patron at the 24-hour diner ordered coffee and spent the entire night staring at a picture, not leaving until dawn. The server approached him after hours of watching him sit there in silence, and when she asked if everything was alright, he replied that he couldn't recall whether the photo was of his own child or belonged to someone else. As he departed, he abandoned the picture on the table, and it remained unclaimed by anyone.

The last customer at the around-the-clock diner sat alone all night with just a cup of coffee, fixated on looking at a photograph. After hours of watching him remain motionless in the corner booth, the waitress finally went over to check on him. When she asked if he was okay, he told her he couldn't remember if the picture showed his own kid or someone else's. He left at sunrise without taking the photo, and nobody ever came back for it.

The waitress kept an eye on him from across the room as he sat motionless in his corner booth for hours. Finally, she approached him and asked if everything was alright. He replied that he couldn't tell if the photo was of his own child or someone else's. As dawn broke, he walked out and left the picture behind, never to return.

He told her he couldn't figure out whether the photo was of his own kid or some other child. When morning came, he got up and left the picture on the table, then walked out and never came back.

The server kept glancing over and saw him frozen in the same booth, unmoving throughout the hours. Eventually she approached and checked if he was alright.
